---
title: "HR prescription helper"
description: "Age, %HRmax, and formula → target bpm; see which published no-BCTT bands your choice overlaps."
tool: true
tool_status: beta
related_installments: [pillar2-002]
date: "2026-06-12"
---

::: {.callout-important}
## Read this first

**Not medical advice. Educational use only.**

- This helper is for the **age-predicted % band path** when symptom-threshold testing (BCTT) is unavailable—not for symptom-only or RPE-only prescription.
- The **bpm output is a ceiling**, not a goal. Stop if symptoms rise **≥2/10** from session baseline.
- Published bands **overlap without converging**; matching a number to a paper does not validate it for your patient.
- Formulae (220−age vs 208−0.7×age) are **not validated in adolescent PPCS** specifically.
:::

## Why this exists

The installment [No treadmill in the room](/posts/pillar2-002-no-bctt-prescription-paths/index.html) argues that the literature does not give one universal %HRmax when BCTT is missing. Parents and clinicians still ask for a **watch-readable number**. This page separates that practical step from the article’s main claim.

**Related reading:** [Figure 2 — overlapping %HRmax bands](/posts/pillar2-002-no-bctt-prescription-paths/index.html#fig-hrmax-overlap) · [A note on the formula](/posts/pillar2-002-no-bctt-prescription-paths/index.html#a-note-on-the-formula-itself)

::: {#fs-hr-calculator .fs-tool-wrap}

<div class="fs-tool-panel">
<div class="fs-tool-controls">
<label class="fs-tool-field">
<span class="fs-tool-label">Age (years)</span>
<input type="range" id="fs-age" min="12" max="18" step="1" value="16">
<output id="fs-age-out" class="fs-tool-value">16</output>
</label>
<label class="fs-tool-field">
<span class="fs-tool-label">Target % age-predicted HR<sub>max</sub></span>
<input type="range" id="fs-pct" min="45" max="75" step="1" value="55">
<output id="fs-pct-out" class="fs-tool-value">55%</output>
</label>
<fieldset class="fs-tool-field fs-tool-formula">
<legend class="fs-tool-label">HR<sub>max</sub> formula</legend>
<label><input type="radio" name="fs-formula" value="220-age" checked> 220 − age (common in literature)</label>
<label><input type="radio" name="fs-formula" value="208-0.7age"> 208 − 0.7 × age (youth-adjusted)</label>
</fieldset>
</div>
<div class="fs-tool-results" aria-live="polite">
<div class="fs-tool-stat">
<strong>Estimated HR<sub>max</sub></strong>
<span id="fs-hrmax">—</span>
</div>
<div class="fs-tool-stat fs-tool-stat-primary">
<strong>Target bpm (ceiling)</strong>
<span id="fs-bpm">—</span>
</div>
<div class="fs-tool-stat">
<strong>Overlaps published bands</strong>
<span id="fs-overlap">—</span>
</div>
</div>
</div>

<div class="fs-tool-chart-wrap">
<svg id="fs-band-chart" class="fs-tool-chart" role="img" aria-label="Published %HRmax bands with your selected percentage"></svg>
</div>

<p class="fs-tool-foot">Dashed brown line on Kozlowski row = ~75% symptom limit (observed); orange bar = RTP guidance &lt;70% when HR<sub>t</sub> is unknown.</p>

:::

## Boundaries

| Topic | What this tool does / does not do |
|-------|-----------------------------------|
| **PPCSexRx** | The CRAN package encodes one published fallback. At age 16 and ~60–70% band its example lands near **133 bpm**—illustration only, not a mandated dose. |
| **Symptom cap** | Always apply ≤2/10 rule from the [pillar2-002 SOP](/posts/pillar2-002-no-bctt-prescription-paths/index.html#sop-checklist-and-educational-tool-layer). |
| **Device** | Wrist optical HR may misread vs chest strap in exercise testing contexts. |
| **Pillar 4** | Fuller “ceiling not goal” checklists will live in future SOP installments; this page stays the calculator. |

[← All tools](/tools/index.html) · [Series hub](/index.html)

::: {=html}
<script src="../_shared/fs-tools.js" defer></script>
:::
